name: Escondida
note: World's largest copper mine by output; BHP 57.5%, Rio Tinto 30%, JECO 12.5%

name: Collahuasi
note: One of the world's largest copper deposits; JV Glencore 44%, Anglo 44%

name: El Teniente
note: World's largest underground copper mine; 100% Codelco (Chilean state)

name: Chuquicamata
note: Historic open-pit; transitioning to underground block cave; Codelco flagship

name: Cerro Verde
note: Major Peruvian open-pit; Freeport 53.6%, Buenaventura 19.6%, Mitsubishi 21%

name: Antamina
note: Large poly-metallic (Cu-Zn); BHP 33.75%, Glencore 33.75%, Teck 22.5%

name: Las Bambas
note: MMG flagship; frequent community road blockades disrupt supply

name: Grasberg (copper)
note: World's largest gold mine by reserves; also top-5 copper; PTFI JV Freeport/Inalum

name: Kamoa-Kakula
note: Fastest-ramping new copper mine; highest-grade large deposit globally; Ivanhoe 39.6%, Zijin 39.6%

name: Tenke Fungurume (copper)
note: Major Cu-Co producer; acquired by CMOC from Freeport 2016; also top cobalt source

name: Oyu Tolgoi (copper)
note: World-class porphyry deposit; underground Hugo North Lift 1 ramping; Rio 50.8% via TRQ

name: Morenci
note: Largest copper producer in North America; Freeport 72%, Sumitomo 28%

name: Buenavista del Cobre (Cananea)
note: Mexico's largest mine; critical LATAM supply node; 100% Grupo Mexico

name: Mount Whaleback (Newman)
note: World's largest single open-cut iron ore mine; anchor of BHP's Pilbara network

name: Hope Downs
note: Part of Rio Tinto's Hamersley Iron operations; JV Rio 50%, Hancock Prospecting 50%

name: Carajas
note: World's largest iron ore mine; highest-grade hematite (~67% Fe); Vale 100%

name: Itabira Complex
note: Historic Iron Quadrangle hub; includes Caue and Conceicao mines; Vale flagship

name: Minas-Rio
note: Slurry pipeline to Acu port; Anglo's sole iron ore asset; high-grade concentrate

name: Kiruna
note: Europe's largest iron ore mine; deep underground; critical for European steel independence

name: Sishen
note: Largest open-cut iron ore mine in Africa; exports via Saldanha Bay; Kumba 74%

name: Weipa
note: World's largest bauxite mine; feeds Gladstone alumina refinery; Rio 100%

name: Boke (Sangaredi / CBG)
note: Guinea holds ~25% of world bauxite reserves; CBG JV: Halco 51%, Guinean state 49%

name: Porto Trombetas (MRN)
note: Major Brazilian bauxite source; JV Vale 40%, Hydro 5%, Alcoa 8.5% + others

name: Sorowako
note: Major laterite nickel producer; 60-year operating history; Vale Indonesia 59.6%

name: Weda Bay (Halmahera IWIP)
note: Fastest-growing HPAL+RKEF complex; Chinese-funded industrial park; dominant in NPI

name: Morowali Industrial Park (IMIP)
note: World's largest nickel-stainless industrial cluster; cornerstone of Indonesia NPI surge

name: Norilsk-Talnakh
note: World's largest producer of refined nickel and palladium; also Co, Cu, Pt; Nornickel 100%

name: Goro (Vale NC)
note: HPAL laterite project; sold by Vale 2021 to Prony Resources; production instability 2024-25

name: Red Dog
note: World's largest zinc mine by output; remote Arctic operation; Teck 100%, NANA regional corp.

name: Mount Isa (zinc-lead)
note: Integrated Zn-Pb-Ag underground mine; part of Glencore's Mount Isa Mines complex

name: Mount Isa (lead-silver)
note: Major lead-silver producer co-located with zinc operations; processed at local smelter

name: Cannington (lead)
note: World's largest single producer of silver and lead; South32 100%

name: Bangka Island (PT Timah)
note: Indonesia is world's largest tin exporter; Bangka offshore and onshore; PT Timah state-owned

name: Wa State (Man Maw mines)
note: Rapid emergence 2022-25 as world's largest new tin source; supply halted Aug 2023 for 6+ months

name: Greenbushes
note: World's highest-grade and largest hard-rock lithium mine; Albemarle 49%, Tianqi 26.01%

name: Salar de Atacama
note: World's largest lithium brine operation; highest Li concentration globally; SQM+Albemarle

name: Hombre Muerto / Fenix
note: High-purity brine; now part of Arcadium Lithium after Livent-Allkem merger 2024

name: Olaroz
note: Allkem's flagship brine; Stage 2 online; JV Arcadium 66.5%, Toyota Tsusho 25%

name: Salar de Uyuni
note: World's largest lithium resource; output constrained by technology and nationalization; JV with CATL

name: Yichun / Jiangxi Lithium Belt
note: China's largest domestic lithium region; lepidolite mica processing ramped up 2022-25

name: Mutanda
note: World's largest cobalt mine; restarted 2022 after care-and-maintenance; Glencore 100%

name: Kamoto (KCC)
note: Underground Cu-Co mine; Glencore 75%, Gecamines 25%; high-grade sulfide deposit

name: Tenke Fungurume (cobalt)
note: Large co-product cobalt from Cu mining; CMOC doubled capacity 2023; Gecamines 20%

name: Heilongjiang Graphite District
note: China dominates ~65% of global natural graphite; Heilongjiang largest district with flake/vein

name: Balama
note: World's largest flake graphite deposit outside China; Syrah 100%; linked to Vidalia AAM plant

name: Bayan Obo
note: World's largest REE deposit; primary Fe mine with RE byproduct; Baogang (BISCO) 100%

name: Mountain Pass
note: Only US rare earth mine and mill; strong in LREE especially Ce, La, Nd; MP Materials 100%

name: Mount Weld
note: Highest-grade known REE deposit; ore processed at Lynas plants Malaysia/Kalgoorlie; 100% Lynas

name: Kalahari Manganese Field
note: World's largest manganese ore reserve (~80% of global); operators inc. Samancor, Assmang

name: Groote Eylandt (GEMCO)
note: Highest-grade open-cut manganese mine globally; South32 60%, Anglo American 40%

name: Moanda (Comilog)
note: One of world's largest Mn mines; Eramet 63.7% via Comilog; key Atlantic supply source

name: Kennecott (Bingham Canyon)
note: Largest man-made excavation on Earth; integrated Cu-Au-Ag-Mo operation; Rio Tinto 100%

name: Spence
note: BHP's Spence Growth Option (SGO) expanded 2021; sulphide concentrator added; BHP 100%

name: Kansanshi
note: Africa's largest copper mine; also significant gold co-product; FQM 80%, ZCCM-IH 20%

name: Sentinel (Trident)
note: Open-pit porphyry copper; FQM's key growth asset in Zambia; FQM 100%

name: Nkomati
note: Only large nickel mine in South Africa; also Cu, Co, Cr, PGE; ARM + Norilsk JV

name: Pilgangoora
note: One of world's largest lithium deposits by resource; Pilbara Minerals 100%

name: Wodgina
note: Restarted 2022; JV MinRes 50%, Albemarle 50%; among world's largest Li deposits

name: Salar del Rincon
note: Rio Tinto acquired Rincon Mining 2022; direct lithium extraction pilot underway

name: Broken Hill
note: Historic Pb-Ag-Zn orebody; 140+ years of mining; South32 operates North Mine

name: Cerro Lindo
note: Peru's largest zinc mine; poly-metallic with Pb, Cu, Ag; Nexa Resources 100%

name: Yandi Mine (Marillana Creek)
note: BHP channel iron deposit in the central Pilbara feeding the Newman-Port Hedland rail and export system.

name: Mining Area C
note: BHP's largest single iron ore hub, incorporating the South Flank development, in the central Pilbara.

name: Jimblebar Mine
note: BHP iron ore hub east of Newman connected to the Port Hedland export rail network.

name: Yandicoogina Mine
note: Rio Tinto's largest single-pit iron ore operation, a channel iron deposit in the central Pilbara.

name: Brockman 4 & Brockman 2
note: Rio Tinto Brockman iron ore mines in the western Pilbara railed to Dampier port.

name: Mount Tom Price Mine
note: Rio Tinto's first Pilbara mine, opened in 1966, in the Hamersley Range.

name: Paraburdoo Mine
note: Rio Tinto iron ore mine in the southern Hamersley Range of the Pilbara.

name: Gudai-Darri (Koodaideri) Mine
note: Rio Tinto's most technologically advanced iron ore mine, commissioned in 2022 in the central Pilbara.

name: West Angelas Mine
note: Rio Tinto Robe River joint-venture iron ore mine in the central Pilbara.

name: Solomon Hub
note: Fortescue iron ore hub (Firetail and Kings deposits) railed to Port Hedland.

name: Chichester Hub (Cloudbreak & Christmas Creek)
note: Fortescue's first iron ore operations in the Chichester Range of the Pilbara.

name: Eliwana Mine
note: Fortescue iron ore mine in the western Pilbara that came online in 2020.

name: Iron Bridge Magnetite Mine
note: Fortescue magnetite operation producing high-grade concentrate via a slurry pipeline to Port Hedland.

name: Roy Hill Mine
note: Hancock Prospecting iron ore mine with dedicated rail and Port Hedland terminal.

name: Cape Lambert Port
note: Rio Tinto iron ore export terminal on the Pilbara coast near Wickham.

name: Dampier Port (Parker Point & East Intercourse)
note: Rio Tinto iron ore export port on the Burrup Peninsula serving the Hamersley mines.

name: Germano / Alegria Complex (Samarco)
note: Samarco iron ore mine in the Iron Quadrangle feeding slurry pipelines to Espirito Santo pellet plants.

name: Vargem Grande Complex
note: Vale iron ore complex in the Southern System of the Iron Quadrangle.

name: Brucutu Mine
note: Vale's largest iron ore mine in the Southeastern System of Minas Gerais.

name: S11D (Serra Sul) Mine
note: Vale's flagship Carajas Serra Sul truckless iron ore mine, the largest in the world by reserves.

name: Ponta da Madeira Maritime Terminal
note: Vale's iron ore export terminal at the end of the Carajas railway in northern Brazil.

name: Thabazimbi Mine
note: Historic South African iron ore mine in Limpopo near the town of the same name.

name: Kolomela Mine
note: Kumba iron ore mine near Postmasburg in the Northern Cape railed to Saldanha Bay.

name: Khumani Mine
note: Assmang iron ore mine near Kathu in the Northern Cape Kalahari iron formation.

name: Bailadila Iron Ore Mines
note: NMDC's flagship high-grade hematite iron ore deposits in the Bailadila range of Chhattisgarh.

name: Donimalai Iron Ore Mine
note: NMDC iron ore mine in the Bellary-Hospet belt of Karnataka.

name: Keonjhar-Joda Iron Ore Belt
note: Major iron ore mining belt in the Keonjhar district of Odisha supplying Indian steelmakers.

name: Barbil-Joda Iron Ore Cluster
note: Dense iron ore mining cluster around Barbil in Odisha's Sundargarh-Keonjhar districts.

name: IOC Labrador City Operations
operator: Iron Ore Company of Canada (Rio Tinto)
note: Rio Tinto-controlled IOC iron ore mine and concentrator in the Labrador Trough.

name: Mont-Wright Mine
note: ArcelorMittal iron ore mine and concentrator near Fermont in northern Quebec.

name: Bloom Lake Mine
note: Champion Iron high-grade iron ore mine near Fermont in the Labrador Trough.

name: Port of Sept-Iles (Pointe-Noire)
note: Major North American iron ore export port on the St. Lawrence serving Labrador Trough mines.

name: Kryvyi Rih Iron Ore Basin (Kryvbas)
note: Ukraine's principal iron ore mining basin around Kryvyi Rih supplying domestic and export markets.

name: Poltava (Horishni Plavni) Mining & Beneficiation
note: Ferrexpo iron ore pellet operation at Horishni Plavni on the Dnieper River.

name: Lebedinsky GOK
note: One of the world's largest iron ore open pits, in the Kursk Magnetic Anomaly.

name: Mikhailovsky GOK
note: Major iron ore mining and beneficiation complex in the Kursk Magnetic Anomaly.

name: Stoilensky GOK
note: NLMK's iron ore mining and processing complex feeding the Novolipetsk steel plant.

name: Malmberget Mine
note: LKAB underground iron ore mine near Gallivare in Swedish Lapland.

name: Port of Narvik (Iron Ore Terminal)
note: Ice-free Atlantic export port for LKAB's Kiruna and Malmberget iron ore via the Ofoten railway.

name: Simandou Iron Ore Project
note: The world's largest untapped high-grade iron ore deposit, under development in southeastern Guinea.

name: El Mutun Iron Ore Deposit
note: One of the world's largest iron ore reserves, on the Bolivia-Brazil border near the Paraguay River.

name: Zouerate (Kedia d'Idjil) Iron Ore Mines
note: SNIM iron ore mines in the Sahara railed ~700 km to the export port of Nouadhibou.

name: Port of Nouadhibou (Cansado)
note: SNIM iron ore export terminal at the end of the Mauritania ore railway.

name: Sierra Grande Iron Ore Mine
note: Argentina's principal iron ore mine on the Patagonian Atlantic coast.

name: Cerro Bolivar / San Isidro Iron Mines
note: State-owned iron ore mines in the Guayana region feeding Orinoco River barges to Puerto Ordaz.

name: Guelb el Rhein (TO14) Mine
note: SNIM magnetite mine and enrichment plant near Fderik in northern Mauritania.

name: Tangshan-Qian'an Iron Ore District
note: Major low-grade magnetite mining district in Hebei supplying China's largest steel cluster.

name: Anshan-Benxi Iron Ore District
note: Historic banded-iron mining district in Liaoning feeding the Ansteel works.

name: Mamatwan Manganese Mine
note: South32 open-pit manganese mine in the Kalahari manganese field near Hotazel.

name: Wessels Manganese Mine
note: South32 underground high-grade manganese mine in the Kalahari field near Hotazel.

name: Woodie Woodie Manganese Mine
note: Open-pit manganese mine in the East Pilbara producing high-grade ore for export.

name: Bootu Creek Manganese Mine
note: Manganese mine north of Tennant Creek in the Northern Territory railed to Darwin.

name: Nsuta Manganese Mine
note: Ghana's principal manganese mine near Tarkwa, exporting through the port of Takoradi.

name: Bondoukou Manganese Mine
note: Manganese mine in northeastern Cote d'Ivoire contributing to West African manganese exports.

name: Bembele Plateau Manganese Mine
note: Comilog manganese extension on the Bembele plateau near Moanda in southeastern Gabon.

name: Okondja (Nouvelle Gabon Mining) Manganese
note: Gabon's second manganese producer near Okondja, exporting via the Transgabonais railway.

name: Chiatura Manganese Mine
note: Historic manganese mining district in central Georgia, in production since the 1870s.

name: Urucum Manganese Mine
note: Manganese deposit in the Urucum massif near Corumba on the Paraguay River.

name: Azul Manganese Mine
note: Vale manganese mine in the Carajas region of Para state.

name: Balaghat Manganese Mine
note: MOIL's deepest manganese mine in the central Indian manganese belt of Madhya Pradesh.

name: Dongri Buzurg Manganese Mine
note: MOIL manganese mine producing dioxide-grade ore in the Bhandara district of Maharashtra.

name: Port of Ngqura (Coega) Manganese Terminal
note: Principal South African manganese export terminal serving the Kalahari field via rail.

name: Owendo Mineral Port (Manganese)
note: Gabon's manganese export terminal at Owendo near Libreville at the end of the Transgabonais railway.

name: Gove Bauxite Mine (Nhulunbuy)
note: Large bauxite mine on the Gove Peninsula feeding seaborne export of the alumina-grade ore.

name: Amrun Bauxite Mine
note: Bauxite mine south of Weipa on Cape York that expanded Rio Tinto's Australian export capacity.

name: Huntly Bauxite Mine
note: One of the world's largest bauxite mines, feeding Alcoa's Pinjarra and Kwinana alumina refineries.

name: Willowdale Bauxite Mine
note: Darling Range bauxite mine supplying the Wagerup alumina refinery in Western Australia.

name: Kamsar / CBG Bauxite Operations
note: CBG mines around Sangaredi rail bauxite to the Kamsar export terminal on Guinea's coast.

name: SMB-Winning Bauxite Operations (Santou/Houda)
note: SMB-Winning consortium runs the world's largest bauxite export operation barging ore to China.

name: Guinea Alumina Corporation Bauxite Mine
note: EGA-owned bauxite mine and rail in the Boke region exporting ore primarily to the UAE.

name: Juruti Bauxite Mine
note: Amazonian bauxite mine in western Para state served by a dedicated rail-and-port system on the Amazon River.

name: Paragominas Bauxite Mine
note: Norsk Hydro bauxite mine that slurry-pipelines ore to the Alunorte alumina refinery near Belem.

name: Alunorte Alumina Refinery (Barcarena)
note: Largest alumina refinery outside China, located at Barcarena near the mouth of the Amazon.

name: Pinjarra Alumina Refinery
note: One of the world's largest alumina refineries, processing Darling Range bauxite in Western Australia.

name: Wagerup Alumina Refinery
note: Western Australian alumina refinery supplied by the nearby Willowdale bauxite mine.

name: Queensland Alumina (QAL) Refinery, Gladstone
note: Major alumina refinery at Gladstone processing Weipa bauxite for global export.

name: Aughinish Alumina Refinery
note: Largest alumina refinery in Europe, on the Shannon Estuary supplying Rusal's smelters.

name: NALCO Damanjodi Alumina Refinery
note: State-owned Indian alumina refinery fed by the adjacent Panchpatmali bauxite plateau.

name: Panchpatmali Bauxite Mine
note: NALCO's plateau bauxite mine in Odisha supplying the nearby Damanjodi refinery.

name: Lanjigarh Alumina Refinery
note: Vedanta alumina refinery in Odisha, one of India's largest, expanding toward 5 Mt/yr.

name: Jamalco Alumina Refinery (Clarendon)
note: Jamaican alumina refinery near Halse Hall processing local Caribbean bauxite.

name: Discovery Bauxite Mine (St Ann)
note: Jamaican bauxite mine exporting ore through Discovery Bay on the north coast.

name: Pingguo Bauxite & Alumina Complex
note: Integrated Chinalco bauxite mining and alumina refining complex in Guangxi.

name: Xiaoyi Bauxite & Alumina District (Shanxi)
note: Major Shanxi alumina refining cluster near Xiaoyi using domestic diasporic bauxite.

name: Weiqiao Binzhou Alumina Refinery
note: Huge Shandong alumina refining base for Hongqiao, largely fed by imported Guinea bauxite.

name: Bintan Alumina Refinery (SGAR)
note: Smelter-grade alumina refinery on Bintan Island processing Indonesian and imported bauxite.

name: Mempawah Smelter-Grade Alumina Refinery
note: State-backed alumina refinery in West Kalimantan built to use domestic bauxite after the export ban.

name: Friguia Alumina Refinery (Fria)
note: Guinea's only alumina refinery, at Fria, processing local bauxite for Rusal.

name: Alliance Mining / Guinea Bauxite Belt (Telimele)
note: Bauxite mining belt in the Telimele district feeding barge exports down the Guinean rivers.

name: Kola MMC - Zapolyarny/Nickel
note: Nornickel's Kola Division mines and processes nickel-copper sulphide ore on the Kola Peninsula near the Norwegian border.

name: Raglan Mine
note: Glencore's Raglan operation mines high-grade nickel-copper sulphide ore in the Cape Smith Belt of northern Quebec.

name: Sudbury Basin Nickel Operations
note: The Sudbury Basin hosts multiple nickel-copper-PGM sulphide mines operated by Vale and Glencore in Ontario.

name: Voisey's Bay Mine
note: Vale's Voisey's Bay operation mines nickel-copper-cobalt sulphide ore in coastal Labrador, transitioning to underground.

name: Thompson Nickel Belt
note: Vale operates underground nickel sulphide mines and a mill along the Thompson Nickel Belt in northern Manitoba.

name: Murrin Murrin Mine
note: Murrin Murrin is a laterite nickel-cobalt mine and HPAL refinery in the Goldfields region of Western Australia.

name: Mount Keith Nickel Mine
note: Mount Keith is a large open-pit nickel sulphide mine in WA's Northern Goldfields, part of BHP's Nickel West (placed on care and maintenance in 2024).

name: Kambalda Nickel District
note: Kambalda is a historic komatiite-hosted nickel sulphide district south of Kalgoorlie in Western Australia.

name: Ravensthorpe Nickel Operation
note: Ravensthorpe is a laterite nickel-cobalt mine and HPAL plant on the south coast of Western Australia.

name: Koniambo Nickel
note: Koniambo mines lateritic ore and smelts ferronickel in New Caledonia's North Province (Glencore withdrew funding in 2024).

name: SLN Doniambo Smelter & Mines
note: SLN operates the historic Doniambo ferronickel smelter at Noumea fed by lateritic ore from several New Caledonia mines.

name: Surigao Nickel Laterite District
note: Surigao del Norte hosts numerous direct-shipping nickel laterite mines that supply ore mainly to Chinese and Japanese smelters.

name: Rio Tuba Nickel Mine
note: The Rio Tuba mine on southern Palawan supplies laterite ore and feeds the adjacent Coral Bay HPAL plant.

name: Tagaung Taung Nickel Mine
note: Tagaung Taung is a laterite nickel mine and ferronickel smelter in central Myanmar developed with Chinese investment.

name: Barro Alto Nickel Mine
note: Barro Alto is a laterite nickel mine and ferronickel plant in Goias state, Brazil, operated by Anglo American.

name: Onca Puma Nickel Mine
note: Vale's Onca Puma operation mines lateritic ore and produces ferronickel in Para state, Brazil.

name: Pomalaa Nickel Operations
note: Antam mines laterite ore and operates ferronickel smelters at Pomalaa in Southeast Sulawesi.

name: Obi Island HPAL (Harita/Halmahera Persada)
note: Obi Island hosts Indonesia's first HPAL plant producing mixed hydroxide precipitate for battery-grade nickel.

name: Kwinana Nickel Refinery
note: The Kwinana refinery near Perth refines nickel and produces battery-grade nickel sulphate (Nickel West suspended in 2024).

name: Antamina Zinc-Copper Mine
note: Antamina is a large polymetallic open-pit mine in Ancash, Peru, and one of the world's biggest zinc producers.

name: Antamina (lead byproduct)
note: Antamina recovers lead as a byproduct alongside its major zinc and copper output in Ancash, Peru.

name: Cerro de Pasco Polymetallic Mine
note: Cerro de Pasco is a historic high-altitude polymetallic mine producing zinc, lead and silver in central Peru.

name: Cerro de Pasco (lead-silver)
note: Cerro de Pasco produces significant lead and silver alongside zinc from its high-altitude polymetallic deposits.

name: San Rafael Tin Mine
note: San Rafael in Puno is the largest underground tin mine in the Americas and the main source of Peruvian tin.

name: Pisco Tin Smelter (Funsur)
note: Funsur's Pisco smelter on Peru's coast refines tin concentrate from San Rafael into LME-grade metal.

name: Huanuni Tin Mine
note: Huanuni is Bolivia's largest underground tin mine, operated by the state mining corporation in Oruro department.

name: Colquiri Tin-Zinc Mine
note: Colquiri is a state-operated tin-zinc mine in La Paz department, Bolivia.

name: Vinto Tin Smelter
note: The Vinto smelter near Oruro is Bolivia's main tin smelter, processing domestic concentrate into refined metal.

name: Kundur Tin Smelter
note: PT Timah's Kundur smelter in the Riau Islands refines tin from Indonesia's offshore and alluvial deposits.

name: Belitung Island Tin District
note: Belitung Island hosts alluvial and offshore tin deposits forming part of Indonesia's main tin-producing belt.

name: Gejiu Tin District (Yunnan Tin)
note: Gejiu in Yunnan is China's tin capital, home to Yunnan Tin, the world's largest tin producer and smelter.

name: Dachang Tin-Polymetallic District
note: The Dachang ore field in Guangxi is a major tin-zinc-antimony polymetallic district in southern China.

name: Bisie Tin Mine (Alphamin)
note: Alphamin's Bisie mine in North Kivu is one of the world's highest-grade tin operations.

name: Manono Tin-Lithium District
note: Manono in Tanganyika province hosts historic and artisanal tin (cassiterite) workings alongside a major lithium deposit.

name: Rutongo Tin Mines
note: Rutongo is a hard-rock cassiterite mine north of Kigali and a key part of Rwanda's tin output.

name: Renison Bell Tin Mine
note: Renison Bell on Tasmania's west coast is Australia's largest tin mine.

name: Mawchi Tin-Tungsten Mine
note: Mawchi in Kayah State is a historic tin-tungsten mine in eastern Myanmar.

name: Rahman Hydraulic Tin Mine
note: Rahman Hydraulic at Klian Intan, Perak is Malaysia's largest operating open-pit tin mine.

name: Malaysia Smelting Corp (Pulau Indah)
note: MSC's Pulau Indah smelter near Port Klang is one of the world's leading custom tin smelters.

name: Rampura Agucha Zinc-Lead Mine
note: Rampura Agucha in Rajasthan is one of the world's largest zinc-lead mines and the core of Hindustan Zinc.

name: Rampura Agucha (lead)
note: Rampura Agucha is a major lead producer alongside its dominant zinc output in Rajasthan, India.

name: Sindesar Khurd Zinc-Lead-Silver Mine
note: Sindesar Khurd in Rajasthan is a major underground zinc-lead-silver mine within the Hindustan Zinc complex.

name: Zawar Mines
note: The Zawar group near Udaipur is one of the oldest zinc-lead mining areas in India.

name: Chanderiya Zinc-Lead Smelter
note: Chanderiya near Chittorgarh is one of the world's largest single-location zinc-lead smelting complexes.

name: Penasquito (zinc-lead)
note: Penasquito in Zacatecas is Mexico's largest mine, producing zinc and lead alongside gold and silver.

name: Penasquito (lead)
note: Penasquito recovers substantial lead alongside its zinc, gold and silver production in Zacatecas, Mexico.

name: San Julian Mine
note: San Julian in Chihuahua is a silver-gold mine that also recovers zinc and lead.

name: Tizapa Polymetallic Mine
note: Tizapa in central Mexico is an underground zinc-lead-silver mine operated with Japanese partner Dowa.

name: Velardena District (Penoles)
note: The Velardena district in Durango produces lead, zinc and silver feeding Penoles' metallurgical operations.

name: Met-Mex Penoles Torreon Smelter
note: Met-Mex Penoles at Torreon is one of the world's largest non-ferrous metallurgical complexes for lead, zinc and silver.

name: McArthur River Zinc-Lead Mine
note: McArthur River in the Northern Territory is one of the world's largest zinc-lead-silver mines.

name: McArthur River (lead)
note: McArthur River produces substantial lead alongside zinc and silver in Australia's Northern Territory.

name: Century Zinc Mine (tailings reprocessing)
note: Century in northwest Queensland reprocesses tailings from the former giant zinc mine via hydraulic mining.

name: Dugald River Zinc Mine
note: Dugald River near Cloncurry in Queensland is a high-grade underground zinc-lead-silver mine operated by MMG.

name: Rosebery Zinc Mine
note: Rosebery on Tasmania's west coast is a long-running underground polymetallic zinc-lead-silver-gold mine.

name: George Fisher Zinc-Lead Mine
note: George Fisher north of Mount Isa is a major underground zinc-lead source feeding Glencore's Mount Isa operations.

name: Huludao Zinc Smelter
note: Huludao in Liaoning is one of China's largest and oldest zinc smelters.

name: Zhuzhou Smelter Group
note: Zhuzhou in Hunan is one of the world's largest zinc smelters and a major lead refiner.

name: Lanping Zinc-Lead Deposit
note: Lanping in Yunnan is one of China's largest zinc-lead deposits, mined by Yunnan Jinding.

name: Shuikoushan Lead-Zinc Mine
note: Shuikoushan in Hunan is a historic lead-zinc mining and smelting district in southern China.

name: Fankou Lead-Zinc Mine
note: Fankou in northern Guangdong is one of China's largest underground lead-zinc mines.

name: Red Dog (lead)
note: Red Dog in northwest Alaska is a leading lead producer alongside its world-class zinc output.

name: Tara Mine (Navan)
note: Tara at Navan is Europe's largest zinc mine, operated by Boliden in County Meath, Ireland.

name: Garpenberg Mine
note: Garpenberg in Dalarna is a highly mechanised underground zinc-lead-silver mine operated by Boliden.

name: Kokkola Zinc Smelter
note: Boliden's Kokkola smelter on Finland's west coast is one of Europe's largest zinc producers.

name: Nordenham Zinc Smelter
note: The Nordenham smelter on the Weser estuary is a major German zinc producer.

name: San Cristobal (zinc-lead)
note: San Cristobal in Potosi is Bolivia's largest mine, producing zinc and lead concentrates rich in silver.

name: San Cristobal (lead)
note: San Cristobal is a major lead and silver producer alongside zinc in Bolivia's Potosi department.

name: Trail Smelter (Teck)
note: Teck's Trail operations in British Columbia form one of the world's largest integrated zinc and lead smelting complexes.

name: Port Pirie Lead Smelter
note: Port Pirie in South Australia is one of the world's largest primary lead smelters.

name: Hobart Zinc Smelter (Risdon)
note: The Risdon smelter near Hobart is a major electrolytic zinc producer in Tasmania.

name: Ridder Zinc Complex (Kazzinc)
note: Ridder in East Kazakhstan is a major polymetallic mining and zinc smelting complex operated by Kazzinc.

name: Ust-Kamenogorsk Zinc-Lead Smelter
note: The Ust-Kamenogorsk complex is Kazakhstan's main integrated zinc and lead refining centre.

name: Gamsberg Zinc Mine
note: Gamsberg in the Northern Cape is one of the world's largest open-pit zinc developments.

name: Skorpion Zinc Mine
note: Skorpion in southern Namibia is a zinc oxide mine and refinery operated by Vedanta.

name: Rosh Pinah Zinc Mine
note: Rosh Pinah in southern Namibia is an underground zinc-lead mine near the Orange River.

name: Perkoa Zinc Mine
note: Perkoa in central Burkina Faso is the country's principal zinc mine.

name: Vazante Zinc Mine
note: Vazante in Minas Gerais is Brazil's largest zinc mine, operated by Nexa Resources.

name: Tres Marias Zinc Smelter
note: The Tres Marias smelter in Minas Gerais is one of South America's largest zinc producers.

name: Aguas Tenidas Mine (Matsa)
note: The Aguas Tenidas complex in Andalusia's Iberian Pyrite Belt is a major Spanish zinc-copper producer.

name: Glogow Copper-Lead Smelter
note: KGHM's Glogow smelter in Lower Silesia recovers lead as a byproduct of copper refining.

name: Olkusz-Pomorzany Zinc-Lead District
note: The Olkusz district near Krakow is Poland's historic zinc-lead mining and smelting area.

name: Pine Point Lead-Zinc District
note: Pine Point on Great Slave Lake is a historic lead-zinc district under redevelopment in Canada's Northwest Territories.

name: Sierra Gorda (zinc byproduct)
note: Sierra Gorda in northern Chile recovers zinc as a byproduct of its large copper-molybdenum operation.

name: El Porvenir Mine (Nexa)
note: El Porvenir in Pasco is an underground polymetallic zinc-lead-silver mine operated by Nexa in central Peru.

name: Cajamarquilla Zinc Refinery
note: Cajamarquilla near Lima is one of the largest zinc refineries in the Americas.

name: Cho Don Lead-Zinc Mine
note: The Cho Don district in Bac Kan is a long-running lead-zinc mining area in northern Vietnam.

name: Monchegorsk Nickel Refinery
note: The Monchegorsk refinery on the Kola Peninsula is Nornickel's main nickel electrolytic refining site.

name: Mount Marion Lithium Mine
note: Hard-rock spodumene mine near Kalgoorlie shipping concentrate mainly to China.

name: Mount Cattlin Lithium Mine
note: Open-pit spodumene operation near Ravensthorpe placed under care and maintenance in 2025.

name: Kathleen Valley Lithium Mine
note: New hard-rock lithium mine in the Northern Goldfields that began production in 2024.

name: Finniss Lithium Project (Grants)
note: Northern Territory spodumene mine near Darwin suspended in 2024 amid low lithium prices.

name: Jianxiawo Lepidolite Mine (Yichun)
note: Major lepidolite lithium mine near Yichun feeding CATL battery supply chains.

name: Jiajika Lithium Mine
note: One of Asia's largest hard-rock spodumene deposits in western Sichuan.

name: Zabuye Salt Lake Lithium
note: High-altitude brine lithium operation on the Tibetan Plateau.

name: Cauchari-Olaroz Lithium Brine
note: Brine lithium carbonate operation in Jujuy that ramped up production from 2023.

name: Centenario-Ratones Lithium Brine
note: Direct lithium extraction brine plant in Salta commissioned in 2024.

name: Sal de Vida Lithium Brine
note: Brine lithium project on the Hombre Muerto salar that started phase-one production in 2024.

name: Salar de Maricunga Lithium Brine
note: High-grade lithium brine salar in northern Chile under state-led development.

name: Thacker Pass Lithium Mine
note: Large sedimentary clay lithium project in Nevada under construction with GM backing.

name: Silver Peak Lithium Operation
note: The only operating lithium brine producer in the United States, in Clayton Valley, Nevada.

name: Manono Lithium Project
note: One of the world's largest undeveloped hard-rock lithium deposits, in southeastern DRC.

name: Goulamina Lithium Mine
note: Hard-rock spodumene mine in southern Mali that began production in late 2024.

name: Bikita Lithium Mine
note: Historic Zimbabwean lithium mine expanded by its Chinese owner for spodumene and petalite.

name: Arcadia Lithium Mine
note: Hard-rock lithium mine near Harare commissioned by Huayou Cobalt in 2023.

name: Mibra Lithium Mine
note: Spodumene mine in Brazil's Lithium Valley in the Jequitinhonha region of Minas Gerais.

name: Grota do Cirilo Lithium Mine
note: Hard-rock spodumene mine in Minas Gerais marketed as low-carbon Triple Zero lithium.

name: Metalkol RTR Tailings Operation
note: Copper-cobalt tailings reprocessing operation at Kolwezi and a major global cobalt source.

name: Kisanfu Copper-Cobalt Mine
note: One of the world's richest copper-cobalt deposits near Kolwezi operated by CMOC.

name: Deziwa Copper-Cobalt Mine
note: Copper-cobalt mine and processing plant near Kolwezi jointly run by CNMC and Gecamines.

name: Kamoa-Kakula Cobalt By-product
note: Tier-one copper complex near Kolwezi that yields cobalt as a by-product.

name: Ruashi Copper-Cobalt Mine
note: Open-pit copper-cobalt mine on the edge of Lubumbashi.

name: Etoile (Shituru) Copper-Cobalt Mine
note: Copper-cobalt mine near Lubumbashi feeding Chemaf's Usoke processing plant.

name: Pomalaa HPAL Cobalt Project
note: High-pressure acid leach project producing mixed hydroxide with cobalt from laterite ores.

name: Huayue HPAL Cobalt Plant (Morowali)
note: HPAL plant in the Morowali industrial park producing nickel-cobalt mixed hydroxide.

name: Murrin Murrin Nickel-Cobalt Mine
note: Laterite nickel-cobalt mine and refinery in the Western Australian Goldfields.

name: Ambatovy Nickel-Cobalt Mine
note: Laterite nickel-cobalt mine in eastern Madagascar with a slurry pipeline to Toamasina.

name: Voisey's Bay Nickel-Cobalt Mine
note: Nickel-copper-cobalt mine in northern Labrador transitioning to underground operations.

name: Molo Graphite Mine
note: Flake graphite mine in southern Madagascar that began production in 2023.

name: Ancuabe Graphite Mine
note: Flake graphite mine in Cabo Delgado province of northern Mozambique.

name: Vohitsara Graphite Mine
note: Flake graphite operation in eastern Madagascar within the Graphmada complex.

name: Lac Knife Graphite Project
note: High-grade crystalline flake graphite deposit in northeastern Quebec.

name: Lac des Iles Graphite Mine
note: Quebec flake graphite mine, one of the few producing natural graphite operations in North America.

name: Vidalia Anode Facility
note: Natural graphite active anode material plant in Louisiana supplied by the Balama mine.

name: Shandong Graphite District (Pingdu)
note: Major Chinese natural flake graphite mining and processing district in Shandong province.

name: Xinghe Graphite District
note: Natural flake graphite mining district at Xinghe in Inner Mongolia.

name: Epanko Graphite Project
note: Flake graphite project in the Mahenge area of central Tanzania.

name: Mahenge Graphite Mine
note: Large high-grade flake graphite deposit at Mahenge in central Tanzania.

name: Santa Cruz Graphite Mine (Pedra Azul)
note: Brazil's main natural flake graphite mining area in northern Minas Gerais.

name: Lynas Advanced Materials Plant (Kuantan)
note: The largest rare-earth separation plant outside China, processing concentrate from Mount Weld.

name: Maoniuping Rare Earth Mine
note: Major bastnaesite light rare-earth mine in the Mianning area of Sichuan.

name: Ganzhou Ionic Clay Rare Earth District
note: Southern China ionic-clay district that dominates global heavy rare-earth supply.

name: Longyan Ionic Clay Rare Earth District
note: Ionic-clay heavy rare-earth mining district in western Fujian province.

name: Kvanefjeld Rare Earth Project
note: Large rare-earth and uranium deposit in southern Greenland stalled by a uranium mining ban.

name: Nolans Rare Earth Project
note: Integrated rare-earth mine and processing project north of Alice Springs in the Northern Territory.

name: Browns Range Rare Earth Project
note: Heavy rare-earth project near the WA-NT border focused on dysprosium and terbium.

name: Eneabba Rare Earth Refinery
note: Integrated rare-earth refinery under construction in Western Australia backed by government finance.

name: Kachin State Rare Earth District
note: Ionic-clay heavy rare-earth mining region in northern Myanmar that feeds Chinese separation plants.

name: Phalaborwa Rare Earth Project
note: Project recovering rare earths from historic phosphogypsum stacks at Phalaborwa.

name: Songwe Hill Rare Earth Project
note: Carbonatite-hosted rare-earth project in southern Malawi targeting neodymium and praseodymium.

name: Serra Verde Rare Earth Mine
note: Ionic-adsorption clay rare-earth mine in Goias that began production in 2024.
